tasks.title: Retrieve relevant Booking.com policies and dispute cases
Search for recent similar complaints and resolutions
Analyze gathered information to identify next steps for complaint triage
Outline recommended actions for complaint handling and guest communication
Compile final triage report and resolution plan

rag_output.summary:
- When a property marks a reservation as a no-show but the guest claims cancellation, support must verify the timeline and escalate if there are conflicts [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show, support should consider the charge potentially refundable and collect cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner details [2].  
- Support must determine who collected payment; if the property charged directly, they need the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before proceeding [3].  

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] refunds_policy.md, [3] payment_disputes.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: When the property marked the reservation as a no-show after the guest claims to have cancelled, support must verify the timeline before declining the request. Timeline conflicts should be escalated.

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the guest cancelled inside the free-cancellation window and the booking was not later marked as a no-show, support should treat the charge as potentially refundable and gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

web_output.summary:
Task: Reporting guests' no-shows and disputing invoices on Booking.com

Public evidence:
- Booking.com provides a process for properties to report guest no-shows to manage overbookings and avoid penalties.
- Hosts can submit no-show reports through their partner account to document and handle such cases.
- There is a procedure for disputing invoices and commissions if hosts find discrepancies or errors in charges from Booking.com.

Sources: [1] https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guests-no-shows-your-property, [2] https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/commission-invoices-tax/invoices/disputing-your-invoice

evidence.title: Reporting guests no-shows at your property - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: You can mark the booking as a no-show. This prevents guests from automatically receiving a satisfaction survey when they didn't stay with you.

evidence.title: Disputing your invoice
evidence.excerpt: If you fail to mark a no-show, guests will still be able to leave a review, which then serves as proof of a completed booking. Read this article ...

evidence.title: Handling cancellation fees and commission - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: A guest doesn't arrive for their booking, you choose to waive the fee and you mark the guest as a no-show either in the extranet or on the Pulse ...

action_plan:
- Verify the guest’s cancellation timeline against the property’s no-show report and cancellation policy, collecting all relevant timestamps, payment details, and communication records.  
- Determine who processed the payment (property or Booking.com); if the property charged directly, request the payment receipt and message trail before proceeding.  
- If conflicts remain after verification, escalate the case internally for resolution and advise the property to use Booking.com’s partner portal to report no-shows or dispute invoices as appropriate.
next_steps:
1. Request and review the guest’s cancellation timestamp, the property’s no-show report, and the applicable cancellation policy to verify the timeline and eligibility.  
2. Confirm who processed the payment; if the property charged directly, obtain the payment receipt and message trail between guest and property.  
3. Compare all collected information to identify any discrepancies or conflicts regarding the cancellation and no-show status.  
4. If unresolved conflicts remain, escalate the case internally for further investigation and advise the property to report no-shows or dispute invoices via the Booking.com partner portal.
final_answer:
Issue:  
A property has marked a reservation as a no-show, but the guest claims they cancelled the booking. This discrepancy requires verification of the cancellation timeline and payment details to determine the correct status and any applicable refunds.

Internal policy:  
- Support must verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation against the property’s no-show report and cancellation policy.  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show, the charge may be refundable. Support should collect the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment ownership details.  
- It is essential to determine who collected the payment. If the property charged the guest directly, support must obtain the payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation timeline before proceeding.  
- Any unresolved conflicts after verification should be escalated internally for resolution.

Public policy:  
- Booking.com allows properties to report guest no-shows through their partner account to manage overbookings and avoid penalties.  
- Hosts can dispute invoices and commissions if they identify errors or discrepancies in charges from Booking.com.  
- These procedures help ensure transparent handling of no-shows and payment disputes.

Recommended action:  
- Collect and review the guest’s cancellation timestamp, the property’s no-show report, and the applicable cancellation policy to verify the timeline.  
- Confirm who processed the payment; if the property charged directly, request the payment receipt and communication records between guest and property.  
- Compare all information to identify discrepancies regarding cancellation and no-show status.  
- If conflicts persist, escalate the case internally and advise the property to use the Booking.com partner portal to report no-shows or dispute invoices as needed.

Next steps:  
1. Request the guest’s cancellation timestamp and the property’s no-show report along with the cancellation policy.  
2. Verify payment ownership and, if applicable, obtain the payment receipt and message trail from the property.  
3. Analyze all collected data to resolve the timeline and payment status.  
4. If the issue remains unresolved, escalate internally and guide the property to report no-shows or dispute invoices via the Booking.com partner portal.

Cited Sources:
- https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guests-no-shows-your-property
- https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/help/reservations/changes-cancellations/handling-cancellation-fees-and-commission
- /Users/ganesamaniankolappan/Documents/Temp_work/Agent/data/rag/refunds_policy.md
- /Users/ganesamaniankolappan/Documents/Temp_work/Agent/data/rag/payment_disputes.md
- https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/commission-invoices-tax/invoices/disputing-your-invoice
